section.heading: Prohibition

content.p: In carrying out this part, the Secretary shall neither mandate nor preclude the use of a particular curricular or pedagogical approach to educating English learners.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 89–10, title III, § 3128, formerly § 3129, as added Pub. L. 107–110, title III, § 301, Jan. 8, 2002, 115 Stat. 1706; renumbered § 3128 and amended Pub. L. 114–95, title III, §§ 3001(2)(B), 3003(l), Dec. 10, 2015, 129 Stat. 1953, 1964.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 3128 of Pub. L. 89–10 was renumbered section 3127 and is classified to section 6848 of this title.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2015—Pub. L. 114–95, § 3003(l), substituted “English learners” for “limited English proficient children”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2015 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 114–95 effective Dec. 10, 2015, except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95, set out as a note under section 6301 of this title.
